title: tikz-standalone
description: A focused standalone TikZ/pgfplots figure: \documentclass{standalone} crops the PDF to a single tightly-bounded figure page. \usepackage{pgfplots} IS a provided-package name in the academic tier, so the §5.4(a) scan preselects + mounts academic BEFORE pass 1 (resolution=scan) — which also makes standalone.cls (academic, invisible to the scan because it is a \documentclass) available for pass 1. The body is a real tikzpicture: an axis with two plotted functions, a title, axis labels and a legend. Distinct from sci-paper (a coordinate line plot embedded in a multi-page paper) by being a single-page cropped figure with function plots. The axis/legend labels render as recoverable pdfTeX text (Frequency/Amplitude/Damping/Response/underdamped/envelope), proving the figure typeset. Exactly one engine pass, no rerun (phases=['engine']).
